articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

article.headline: Enzo Fernandez sent off as Argentina rally for Colombia draw
article.description: Argentina rescued a 1-1 draw at home to Colombia in the Conmebol World Cup qualifiers on Tuesday night, after Liverpool's Luis Díaz scored a sensational individual goal to stun the world champions.
article.linkText: Enzo sent off as Argentina rally for Colombia draw

images.name: Luis Díaz scores stunning solo goal in Colombia's draw vs. Argentina
images.caption: Luis Díaz scores a brilliant goal for Colombia in a 1-1 draw with Argentina.

article.story:
Argentina rescued a 1-1 draw at home to Colombia in the Conmebol World Cup qualifiers on Tuesday night, after Liverpool's Luis Díaz scored a sensational individual goal to stun the world champions.

Diaz weaved past several Argentina players before firing past goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez to hand struggling Colombia a shock lead with 24 minutes played.

Chelsea midfielder Enzo Fernández was then sent off in the second half for a high boot that landed a bloody blow on Kevin Castaño's forehead, before a subdued Lionel Messi was replaced.

Thiago Almada drew Argentina level with just nine minutes remaining when he fired into the bottom corner beyond Kevin Mier's desperate dive.

The point keeps Colombia sixth in the hunt to secure an automatic qualifying spot, while extending their winless streak to six games.

Argentina remain top of the CONMEBOL qualification table with 35 points from 16 games, while rivals Brazil secured their spot at the World Cup with a win over Paraguay.

Information from The Associated Press was used in this recap.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! Argentina 0, Colombia 1. Luis Díaz (Colombia) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Kevin Castaño following a fast break.

Kevin Castaño (Colombia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, Argentina 0, Colombia 1.

Daniel Muñoz (Colombia) Yellow Card at 93'
